rationale: Roehm et al. (2002) is cited in the discussion as part of the loyalty-program success-factor literature that this paper claims to advance. The citing paper explicitly contrasts prior emphasis on program design features with its own finding that customer self-determination and customer selection materially shape loyalty-program outcomes. This gives the cited work a clear positioning role in the paper's claimed contribution, not just a passing background mention.
relation_description: The citing paper extends the loyalty-program research stream represented by Roehm et al. (2002) by adding customer self-determination as a key boundary condition for program effectiveness. It argues that, beyond incentive design, enrolling the right customers drives stronger relational outcomes.
